name: Build Aggregation Pipelines from CLI
description: Construct and test MongoDB aggregation pipelines in mongosh for data transformation, grouping, and analysis directly from the command line.

steps.title: Basic match and group pipeline
steps.description: Filter documents and group them to compute aggregated values.

steps.title: Use $lookup for collection joins
steps.description: Join data from another collection similar to a SQL LEFT JOIN.
steps.command: mongosh --eval 'db.orders.aggregate([{$lookup: {from: "customers", localField: "customerId", foreignField: "_id", as: "customer"}}, {$unwind: "$customer"}, {$project: {orderId: 1, amount: 1, customerName: "$customer.name"}}, {$limit: 5}])' mydb

steps.title: Date-based aggregation with $group
steps.description: Group orders by month to analyze trends over time.

steps.title: Use $facet for multiple aggregations in one query
steps.description: Run several aggregation pipelines in parallel within a single query.
steps.command: mongosh --eval 'db.orders.aggregate([{$facet: {byStatus: [{$group: {_id: "$status", count: {$sum: 1}}}], topCustomers: [{$group: {_id: "$customerId", total: {$sum: "$amount"}}}, {$sort: {total: -1}}, {$limit: 5}], dailyRevenue: [{$group: {_id: {$dateToString: {format: "%Y-%m-%d", date: "$createdAt"}}, revenue: {$sum: "$amount"}}}, {$sort: {_id: -1}}, {$limit: 7}]}}])' mydb
steps.tip: $facet runs all sub-pipelines on the same input dataset, making it efficient for dashboard-style queries.

steps.title: Explain an aggregation pipeline
steps.description: Analyze the execution plan to understand how MongoDB processes the pipeline.

faq.question: What is the maximum size of an aggregation pipeline result?
faq.answer: By default, each document in the pipeline cannot exceed 16MB (BSON limit). The total result returned to the client is also limited to 16MB. For large results, use $out or $merge to write results to a collection, or use a cursor.

faq.question: Does $match use indexes?
faq.answer: Yes, but only when $match is at the beginning of the pipeline (or right after $sort). Place $match as early as possible to filter documents before they enter more expensive pipeline stages.

faq.question: How do I save an aggregation result to a new collection?
faq.answer: Add {$out: 'result_collection'} or {$merge: {into: 'result_collection'}} as the last pipeline stage. $out replaces the target collection entirely. $merge can insert, update, or merge into an existing collection.
